description: A rigorous, fully explicit truncation-error analysis (as in Duarte–Koch 2020 or comparable) has not been performed. The experiment provides numerical estimates σ_p ≥ 0.530 for p ≤ 31 at N=40, but a certified error bound transporting these to the infinite-dimensional limit is absent. To fix: run explicit interval arithmetic on the Galerkin matrix evaluation and reproduce with strict rounding error control, or apply an existing rigorous a-posteriori error bounding method.
action_taken: Interval arithmetic certification via arb (256-bit ball arithmetic) has been performed on the finite Galerkin matrix (certify_rho_arb.py, commit 9428528), certifying ρ_η ≤ 0.7606. The gap between the certified Galerkin spectrum and the full operator spectrum is explicitly documented as an open limitation. The finding page does not claim rigorous operator-level bounds.
